remember: Students will be able to recall the definition and types of coordination compounds.
Students will be able to identify and write the IUPAC names of coordination compounds.
Students will be able to list the characteristics of coordination compounds.
understand: Students will be able to explain the formation of coordination compounds and the role of ligands.
Students will be able to describe the geometry and isomerism in coordination compounds.
Students will be able to explain the magnetic properties of coordination compounds.
apply: Students will be able to apply the VSEPR theory to predict the geometry of coordination compounds.
Students will be able to calculate the oxidation number of the central metal atom in a coordination compound.
Students will be able to predict the magnetic behavior of a coordination compound based on its structure.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the structure of a coordination compound and predict its physical and chemical properties.
Students will be able to compare the properties of different coordination compounds and explain the reasons for their differences.
Students will be able to evaluate the importance of coordination compounds in various fields, such as medicine and catalysis.

ncert_objectives: To understand the types of coordination compounds and their IUPAC nomenclature.
To explain the formation of coordination compounds and the role of ligands.
To describe the geometry and isomerism in coordination compounds.
board_exam_objectives: To identify and write the IUPAC names of coordination compounds.
To describe the characteristics and properties of coordination compounds.
To explain the magnetic properties and applications of coordination compounds.
